REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT JEFFREY BLOOM, ELAINE SIERRA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFFS MUST ALLEGE IN WHICH CAPACITY EACH DEFENDANT IS BEING SUED AND ALLEGE EACH ELEMENT OF EACH CAUSE OF ACTION (INCLUDING DAMAGES) AGAINST EACH DEFENDANT (IF DEFENDANTS ARE BEING SUED IN MULTIPLE CAPACITIES ALLEGATIONS MUST BE MADE TO SUPPORT A CAUSE OF ACTION AS TO EACH). DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS ARGUMENT AND SUFFICIENCY OF ALLEGATIONS REGARDING DEFECTIVE CONDITIONS. THE COURT NOTES THAT PARTIES FAILED TO MEET AND CONFER IN GOOD FAITH. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL CHOSE NOT TO STRICTLY COMPLY WITH THE MEET AND CONFER REQUIREMENTS, BUT SENT A DETAILED LETTER. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL CHOSE TO ADHERE TO STRICT COMPLIANCE AND IGNORED THE DETAILED LETTER. THESE ATTITUDES SHOW LACK OF GOOD FAITH AND UNDERMINE THE PURPOSE OF MEET AND CONFER REQUIREMENTS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-17-2018 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-09-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JEFFREY BLOOM, ELAINE SIERRA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Appearances: Julian T. Lastowski of Sheppard Uziel Law Firm (415.296.0900 x32) for defendants and moving parties; Noah Woo of Bracamontes & Vlasak (415.835.6777) for plaintiff and responding party.

This matter is not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiffs must allege in which capacity each defendant is being sued and allege each element of each cause of action (including damages) against each defendant (if defendants are being sued in multiple capacities allegations must be made to support a cause of action as to each). Demurrer is overruled as to the Statute of Limitations argument and sufficiency of allegations regarding defective conditions. The Court notes that parties failed to meet and confer in good faith. Defendant's counsel chose not to strictly comply with the meet and confer requirements, but sent a detailed letter. Plaintiff's counsel chose to adhere to strict compliance and ignored the detailed letter. These attitudes show lack of good faith and undermine the purpose of meet and confer requirements. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/REQ)

Motion To Strike Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Jul-09-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES.

Appearances: Julian T. Lastowski of Sheppard Uziel Law Firm (415.296.0900 x32) for defendants and moving parties; Noah Woo of Bracamontes & Vlasak (415.835.6777) for plaintiff and responding party.

This matter is not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: moot. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/REQ)
